description: Per-file comment/doc counts for LLM batch targeting. Rust: lines_triple_slash=///, lines_inner_doc=//!, lines_plain_comment=// excluding doc. Markdown: lines_total=all lines; lines_other_doc_signal=# heading count. hotspot_tier: 1=plan-listed path, 2=high-density heuristic, 0=other. symbol_hints (schema v3): plan-hotspot Rust only; /// or //! linked to next item; containing_symbol, doc_preview, comment_type, quality_tag (mechanical|operational|section_divider|user_help|narrative|ssot_sensitive).

hints.doc_preview: Expression AST: literals, control flow, calls, JSX, blocks.
Expressions may contain [`crate::stmt::Stmt`] sequences only inside `Expr::Block`, `Expr::If`
branches, and similar nodes. The parser does not distinguish “statement context” vs “expression
context” in the type system—that distinction is enforced during typechecking.
**Tooling:** Prefer matching on [`Expr`](crate::expr::Expr) variants and using embedded
diagnostics; do not assume expression shapes mirror HIR 1:1 until after lowering.
Call argument: optional label (`name = value`) plus value expression.
Infix operators in surface syntax (including `is` / `isnt` for reference equality).
A match arm: pattern [if guard] -> body
Span covering the opening tag through closing tag.
Self-closing JSX: tag and attributes, then `/>` with no separate closing tag.
Span covering the self-closing tag through `/>`.
A JSX attribute: name={expr} or name="string"
Attribute value expression or string literal.
Default value when omitted at call sites.
Span covering the parameter in the signature.
Resolved textual name as written in source.
Field name / value pairs in source order.
Binary expression: `a + b`, `a and b`, `a is b`
Span covering the full binary expression.
`match subject: arm -> body …` — value is the chosen arm’s body (all arms must be compatible).
Arms: pattern, optional `if` guard, `->` body expression.
Conditional: `if cond:` then-branch stmts, optional `else:` branch.
For expression (used in JSX): `for x in list: <elem>`
Span covering the entire string literal.
Braced sequence of statements in an expression position (e.g. after `=>`).

Concrete HIR types: one lowered module with separate vectors per top-level construct.
**Names:** Identifiers and resolved paths in [`HirExpr`] / patterns reflect the lowering pass
([`crate::lower`]), not necessarily raw source spelling. Prefer [`vox_ast::span::Span`] on each node for
**Consumers:** `vox-typeck` and codegen read these types; keep new variants backward-compatible
or bump all match sites in the same change.
Unique identifier for definitions within a module.
Opaque numeric id assigned during HIR lowering.
A fully lowered Vox module: every declaration category is collected into its own vector.
Empty vectors mean the construct was absent in source; there is no implicit ordering across
categories—only within each `Vec` matches source order for that kind.

Parameters with optional types and defaults.
Expression in HIR (mirrors AST but with resolved names).
Call; `bool` indicates tail-call hint when used by backend.

fan-out with [`KnowledgeQueryParams::limit`](crate::memory::KnowledgeQueryParams::limit) so tool responses stay bounded. For authoritative

turn addition also writes to the `user_sessions` and `session_turns`
tables. JSONL files remain the hot cache; VoxDB is the durable SSOT.
Optional VoxDB backing store for SSOT persistence.

Core orchestrator value types: ids, tasks, file affinity, and bulletin messages.
These structs serialize cleanly for dashboards and Codex snapshots; prefer them over
ad-hoc tuples when crossing crate boundaries.
Unique identifier for a task within the orchestrator.
Unique identifier for an agent within the orchestrator.
Unique identifier mapping a question and response together.
Unique identifier for a batch submission
Handle for an active lock on a resource
Thread-safe counter for generating sequential TaskIds.
Returns the next monotonic task id.
Thread-safe counter for generating sequential AgentIds.
Returns the next monotonic agent id.
Thread-safe counter for generating sequential CorrelationIds.
Returns the next monotonic correlation id for Q/A pairing.
Priority level for a task. Higher priority tasks are dequeued first.
Current execution status of a task.
Waiting in the queue to be picked up.
Currently being executed by an agent.
Blocked waiting for another task to complete.
Kind of access an agent requires on a file.
Read-only access (multiple agents can hold simultaneously).
Exclusive write access (only one agent at a time).
A file path paired with the access kind required for a task.

Request parameters, row shapes, and errors for [`crate::store::CodeStore`] (Arca on Turso).
**SSOT:** Schema versions live under [`crate::schema`]. Public Codex naming and env vars are
defined in ADR 004 and the repo-root `AGENTS.md`—do not restate deployment semantics on
every struct; link those docs when adding new persisted fields.
**Pattern:** Parameter structs that mirror a `CodeStore::…` method include a rustdoc link to that
method so call sites stay discoverable.
Parameters for logging an execution (workflow / activity).
Optional JSON or opaque options string.
Wall-clock latency in milliseconds, if recorded.
JSON tool manifest or config, if any.
Whether the agent is visible to other users.
Store operation failure (Turso, not-found, or serialization).
Requested row or binding was missing.

User or agent id the pattern belongs to.

Count inferred as successful from metadata heuristics.
Average duration in ms when present in metadata.
Prompt/response pair plus optional human feedback.

HF file checks before native Candle QLoRA (`vox populi train --backend qlora`).
**Tokenizer contract:** `--backend qlora` requires [`PopuliTokenizerMode::Hf`] and a real
Hugging Face `tokenizer.json` (download via `--model <repo>` or explicit path). The Burn LoRA
path (`--backend lora`) uses the Vox tokenizer stack — do not mix modes; CLI enforces this before
**Weights:** requires HF `config.json` plus safetensors shards listing a supported **embedding**
matrix (`wte.weight` or `model.embed_tokens.weight`) for vocab / `d_model` discovery passed into
[`super::candle_qlora_train`]. Full training graph details: see module docs on
Prefer `wte` (GPT-2) before `embed_tokens` (Llama/Qwen) when both exist anywhere in the shard list.
Scan all shards: first **valid** rank-2 table in key order (`wte` then `embed_tokens`).
If a preferred key exists but is not rank-2, fail (do not fall back silently).
Resolved embedding table metadata for the Candle trainer.
HF `config.json` path (used for layout parse and tracing; keep for operator debugging).
Fail fast unless HF tokenizer + safetensors shards contain a supported embedding matrix.
